rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. VS. COLUMBUS JAZZ, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For An Order Approving The Dibursement Of Funds Held In Trust
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 12, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INCORPORATED'S MOTION For An Order Approving The Dibursement Of Funds Held In Trust Pro Tem Judge Rolando Pasquali, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied without prejudice. Neither the Forbearance Agreement nor the June 27, 2011 order by The Honorable Loretta Giorgi in San Francisco Superior Court Case Number CGC 11-511390, "CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. vs. RICHARD B. COLE" permit the relief sought in this motion. It does not appear, nor does Plaintiff submit proof of, compliance with the June 27, 2011 order in case CGC 11-511390 that Plaintiff "file pleadings" within 45 days to remove and replace Mr. Cole as receiver "in any cases" that it was aware of in which Mr. Cole was a receiver. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to lawyer@netwiz.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: SALVATORE MINEO INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL et al VS. CITY SIGHTSEEING CORPORATION, A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Salvatore Mineo Individually, And On Behalf Of All, Gina Schembari Individually , And On Behalf Of All, Phillip Nails, Benjamin Daux, David Hayward'S Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 12, 2012, Line 4, Plaintiffs Salvatore Mineo Individually, And On Behalf Of All, Gina Schembari Individually , And On Behalf Of All, Phillip Nails, Benjamin Daux, David Hayward'S Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Pro Tem Judge Rolando Pasquali, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to complete the deposition is granted. As to the number of depositions, counsel are referred to CCP 2025.610(c)(1). The court will not place a time limit upon the length of the deposition; however, this should not be interpreted as license to engage in repetitive, protracted, or abusive questioning. Sanctions are denied; defense counsel was able to proceed on January 25, 2012. Moving party's attention is directed to California Rules of Court, Rules 3.1112(d)(3) and 3.1113(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to lawyer@netwiz.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND GARCIA VS. BENITA RAMOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Or Modify Deft'S Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records; Req. For Sanctions Against Deft Benita Ramos And His Attorney David Semel; Dec
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 12, 2012, Line 15, PLAINTIFF RAYMOND GARCIA's Motion To Quash Or Modify Defendant's Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records; Request For Sanctions Against Defendantt Benita Ramos And His Attorney David Semel - The motion is granted in part. Defendant may serve a subpoena for only those records that directly reflect payment of rent by RADCO on behalf of Plaintiff Raymond Garcia for the premises located at 38 Pope St. The need for such records would be obviated if the parties stipulated that RADCO paid two month rent for Plaintiff. The court notes that the caveat that Plaintiff's counsel would include in the stipulation and that apparently caused it to founder, i.e. "without any prejudice to any arguments we may make in this matter at trial" is not properly a stipulated fact and has little or no meaning without context. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The court encourages the parties to finalize their stipulation. =(302/JJM)
